sourceMovie.title: MadS
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit MadS for the same reason it broke through in 2024: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Moreau keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Get Out
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If MadS clicked for you, Get Out (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after MadS?
faq.answer: Try Get Out, Dressed to Kill, Don't Hang Up—each shares DNA with MadS while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is MadS?
faq.answer: MadS (2024) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is MadS worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
